summary: Excellent soundboard recording captures a rock‑inspired show highlighted by a stellar Half Step → Franklin’s Tower opening and soaring China Doll/Playing in the Band jam.
review: Fans rave about the crystal‑clear SBD tape, calling the sound “out of this world.” The performance shines especially in the second set with a powerful Half Step > Franklin’s Tower transition, a fiery China Doll into Playing in the Band, and an inspiring Space. Overall the concert is praised as one of the best from 1986.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both musical performance and recording quality across four detailed reviews; no significant negatives reported.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, out‑of‑this‑world sound with no dropout or balance issues mentioned; reviewers specifically note the SBD quality.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set praised for its pacing (Iko Iko start, Bertha > Sat Night close). Second set described as “very rockin,” highlighted by the Half Step > Franklin’s Tower opener and a seamless China Doll > Playing in the Band jam before Drum solo.
